intro: Cereus biolleyi, also known as the Guatemalan prickly pear cactus, is a species of cactus native to Central America, belonging to the Cereus genus of columnar cacti. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cereus biolleyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin wounds and infections.
